2) The click handler reads the current About page URL parameters (for example, section and audience) from the current URL.
3) The handler constructs a navigation URL for contact.html with optional query parameters (such as source=about and contextSection set to the current section).
4) The browser navigates to contact.html with the constructed query string, allowing the Contact page to know the user came from About and which context they were in.

Workflow: See cart status and proceed to cart from the About page
1) User opens about.html; on page load, the header cart summary component calls getCartSummary.
2) The component displays the returned item_count and subtotal values as a compact cart status.
3) If the user clicks the cart icon or the summary section, the click handler navigates to the cart page URL (for example, cart.html) without modifying About page URL parameters.
4) When the user returns to about.html (for example, via browser back), the cart summary component calls getCartSummary again to refresh the displayed item_count and subtotal.
interactions:
Interaction: Page load
- Event: Window load or DOM ready on about.html.
- Actions: Invoke getAboutPageContent to retrieve About content; invoke getCartSummary to retrieve cart summary; initialize URL-state synchronization based on current query parameters (such as section, audience, showCertifications).

Interaction: Section selection
- Trigger: User clicks a section entry in the internal section navigation or in the body sections list.
- Actions: Update the active section in page state; scroll the corresponding section into view; update the section query parameter in the URL using the History API (without full reload).

Interaction: Customer type selection
- Trigger: User clicks a customer type item in the customer types list.
- Actions: Mark the clicked customer type as selected in state; update the audience query parameter in the URL; optionally ensure the relevant explanatory content in body_sections is visible or emphasized.

Interaction: Certifications visibility toggle
- Trigger: User interacts with a control to expand or collapse the certifications list.
- Actions: Toggle an isCertificationsExpanded flag in state; update or remove the showCertifications query parameter in the URL to reflect the current visibility state.

Interaction: Contact call-to-action click
- Trigger: User clicks the Contact call-to-action element on the About page.
- Actions: Read the current section and audience values from either state or the URL; construct a navigation URL for contact.html that includes source=about and optional context parameters (for example, contextSection and contextAudience); set window.location to the constructed URL.

Interaction: Cart summary refresh
- Trigger: Page load on about.html and any custom cart-updated event dispatched globally by other pages after cart changes.
- Actions: Invoke getCartSummary; update header cart summary state with item_count, subtotal, and currency; re-render the cart summary display.

Interaction: Cart summary click
- Trigger: User clicks the cart summary area or cart icon in the header.
- Actions: Navigate to the cart page URL (such as cart.html) without altering About-specific query parameters.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The About page itself does not require any fixed incoming URL parameters, but it can respond to optional query parameters to control initial and ongoing state. Supported optional parameters include:
- section: Used to identify which About body section should be active or scrolled into view on load. The value is treated as a slug that can be matched against body_sections entries derived from getAboutPageContent (for example, by normalizing each title to a slug on the client). On load, if section is present and matches an available section slug, that section is set as active. When the user selects a different section via the UI, the section parameter is updated with history.replaceState or history.pushState.
- audience: Used to indicate a selected customer type. After getAboutPageContent resolves, the customer_types list is checked for a value that can be matched to the audience parameter (for example, by slug or normalized label). If a match is found, it is set as selected in state, and the UI emphasizes it. When the user clicks another customer type, the audience parameter is updated accordingly. If no match is found, no customer type is preselected.
- showCertifications: A boolean-like parameter (presence or a specific value) used to determine whether the certifications block should be expanded by default. On load, if showCertifications indicates true, the page state sets the certifications block as expanded. When the user toggles the block, the parameter is updated (added or removed) to reflect the current state.
These parameters are read from location.search on initial load and on popstate events (browser back/forward). Internal interactions that change section, audience, or showCertifications update the URL without full page reload, ensuring that sharing the URL or reloading the page reconstructs the same About page state. No state is stored in localStorage; all shareable state is either kept in memory for the current session or encoded into URL parameters. The getAboutPageContent and getCartSummary interfaces are idempotent with respect to these URL parameters: they are called based on page lifecycle, not directly on parameter changes, while parameter changes impact only how the returned data is presented.

components.functionality: On page initialization, this component invokes the getAboutPageContent interface, stores the returned headline, body_sections, customer_types, and certifications in a dedicated AboutPageState object, and notifies dependent components that content is available.

Renders the About page headline and body sections from AboutPageState. Determines which section is active based on the section URL parameter on load and updates the URL when the user selects a different section. Handles scrolling or focusing behavior to keep the active section in view.

Provides an internal navigation list of the available body sections derived from AboutPageState.body_sections. When a section label is clicked, it sets that section as active in shared state and synchronizes the section URL parameter, without re-fetching data.

Displays the customer_types array from AboutPageState to clarify which customer segments are served. Reads the audience parameter on load to determine which customer type should be selected initially. Clicking a customer type updates the selected audience in state and updates the audience URL parameter.

Shows the list of certifications from AboutPageState.certifications. Uses a local isExpanded flag that is initialized based on the showCertifications URL parameter. When the user toggles expansion, the isExpanded state is updated and the showCertifications parameter in the URL is synchronized.

Renders a primary call-to-action that navigates to contact.html. On click, reads the current section and audience values from either AboutPageState or URL parameters, then constructs a destination URL for contact.html that includes source=about and any relevant context parameters before performing navigation.

On page load and whenever a global cart-updated event is received, this component calls getCartSummary to obtain the current item_count, subtotal, and currency, then displays them as a compact status in the header. When the user clicks the cart summary area, it navigates to the cart page.

Centralizes reading and writing of URL parameters relevant to the About page (section, audience, showCertifications). On initial load and on popstate events, it updates shared state (for example, active section, selected audience, certifications expansion). When internal state changes through user interactions, it updates URL parameters using the History API without triggering a page reload.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid typical of WooCommerce/WordPress themes, with content centered in a fixed-width container inside a full-bleed header.
layout.layout_pattern: Reuse the global e‑commerce shell from the mockup (black utility bar → yellow main header with logo/nav/cart → full-width search/category bar → breadcrumb row), then swap the product-title and category-filter area for an About-page hero and internal section navigation. Below that, replace the product grid with vertically stacked About content blocks: company story, who we serve, certifications, and a prominent contact CTA. All main About content sits in a centered, fixed-width container that follows a 12-column grid, with the text column slightly narrower than full width to preserve generous side margins.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Reuse the global e‑commerce shell from the mockup (black utility bar → yellow main header with logo/nav/cart → full-width search/category bar → breadcrumb row), then swap the product-title and category-filter area for an About-page hero and internal section navigation. Below that, replace the product grid with vertically stacked About content blocks: company story, who we serve, certifications, and a prominent contact CTA. All main About content sits in a centered, fixed-width container that follows a 12-column grid, with the text column slightly narrower than full width to preserve generous side margins.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The combined header area (black utility bar + yellow main header + search/category bar) still occupies roughly the top 20–25% of the viewport. The About hero band (breadcrumb + whitespace + page title + internal section navigation) uses another ~20–25% with intentionally airy spacing. The lower ~50–60% is dedicated to the About content stack: main narrative, customer types list, certifications, and CTA, arranged in a single central column that spans about 8–10 of 12 columns. A slim right margin is visually kept free so that the persistent chat widget and WhatsApp FAB (global elements) can overlay without feeling cramped.
layout_strategies.content_density: The upper portion (header, search bar, breadcrumb, title, section navigation) maintains low-to-moderate density with generous vertical gaps (24–48px) between rows and minimal text per band. The central About content is moderately dense, with paragraphs and lists readable and spaced in 16–24px vertical steps. The customer types and certifications lists are visually denser—akin to a product grid—using multi-column layouts and tighter intra-item spacing while preserving 24–32px breathing room between sections. The final contact CTA returns to a low-density style, using a bold coloured panel and ample padding so it stands out and feels uncluttered.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The page keeps a Z-pattern flow: the eye starts at the top-left logo in the black/yellow header and tracks right to the navigation and cart summary, then drops diagonally to the left side where the breadcrumb and page title anchor the About content. From the centered title, the eye moves right across the internal section navigation links, then down into the left-aligned About content column, scanning down through the main narrative, then horizontally across the customer types and certifications grids. The strong contact CTA near the bottom forms the final focal block. Throughout, the right-side chat overlay and WhatsApp FAB act as secondary attention hooks but do not interrupt the main left-to-right content reading path.

layout.overall_description: The About page sits inside the same clean, corporate e‑commerce shell as the product pages. A thin black utility bar runs full-width at the very top, followed immediately by a bold yellow header band with the logo on the left, navigation links through the center, and a compact cart summary on the right. Beneath it, a full-width search/category bar stretches edge-to-edge, while the main content is constrained to a centered, fixed-width container that never touches the viewport edges. A light breadcrumb row appears first in this container, aligned to the left, followed by a generous vertical gap and a large, centered About page title. Directly under the title, a single-row internal section navigation mimics the product category filter: horizontally arranged text tabs centered in the page, with one active state. Below this hero band, the layout transitions to a left-aligned content column that occupies roughly two-thirds to four-fifths of the container width, leaving clear side gutters. The main About narrative flows first, in wide but readable paragraphs, then gives way to a multi-column ‘Who we serve’ section and a similarly structured certifications area, both echoing the denser feeling of a product grid. At the bottom of the content column, a prominent, generously padded contact CTA card spans most of the column’s width and visually anchors the page. Overlaid independently of this flow, a right-side chat panel and a bottom-right floating WhatsApp button (from the global system) sit above the content, making the right edge visually active yet not obstructive. The overall impression is clean, structured, and easy to scan—corporate but approachable, with clear hierarchy and breathable whitespace.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The content loader is positioned within the main About content container, overlaying only the area below the hero band (page title + internal section navigation). It appears as a semi-transparent white overlay or centered loader element that covers the left-aligned content column where the main narrative, customer types list, certifications, and contact CTA would normally render. Horizontally, the overlay aligns with the main content column edges (approximately 8–10 of 12 grid columns centered on the page), leaving the outer margins, header, and right-side chat/WhatsApp overlay untouched. Vertically, it begins about 24–32px below the internal section navigation and extends down to the fold or until the end of the loaded content region. When active, the rest of the layout (header, search bar, breadcrumb, title, and section navigation) remains visible to maintain orientation; only the primary reading column displays skeleton states or a spinner, consistent with the clean, minimal visual style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as an absolute-positioned overlay anchored to the main content container; it scrolls with the page, appearing and disappearing based on data loading state, without affecting the global header or floating chat/WhatsApp elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The main content area sits below the hero band, starting approximately 40–48px beneath the internal section navigation to preserve the open mid-page feel. It is rendered as a single vertical stack of text and visual blocks, aligned to the left within a centered container that spans around 8–10 of the 12 grid columns (roughly 60–70% of the viewport width on large screens). The left edge of this column aligns with the breadcrumb and H1 alignment above, creating a strong vertical rhythm; the right edge leaves a generous margin that keeps line lengths readable and allows the overlay chat panel on the far right to feel separate. Within this area, the primary company background and expertise narrative appears first, using short paragraphs and possible subheadings. Each sub-section maintains 16–24px spacing between paragraphs and 24–32px between subsection headings, adhering to the 8px baseline. The main content sets the tone for the lower modules; it visually transitions into the customer types list by adding a 32–48px gap below its last paragraph, signaling a section break while sustaining the clean, corporate aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level section within the scrolling document flow; it resizes responsively with the main container but remains centered and left-aligned, without sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The internal section navigation is positioned directly below the central H1 title in the hero area, acting as the About-page analogue to the product category filter. Horizontally, it spans the full width of the fixed content container but visually groups its links in a single row centered on the page. Each nav item appears as a text link or subtle pill, with 16–24px horizontal spacing between labels and 8–12px vertical padding inside each clickable area, following the 8px grid. The row sits roughly 16–24px beneath the page title, with ample whitespace above and below so it reads as a distinct, lightweight control band. The active section (e.g., ‘Overview’, ‘Who we serve’, ‘Certifications’) is indicated via brand colour underlines or a highlight, mirroring the subtle emphasis of the category filter in the product mockup. On wider screens it occupies visually about the central 50–60% of the container, with equal left/right breathing room to keep the layout airy and balanced.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Initially part of the normal flow between the title and content; optionally configured as sticky within the content container so that after the user scrolls past the hero area, this navigation bar pins to the top of the viewport, just below the global header, providing persistent section access without overlapping the chat widget.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The customer types list is introduced as a distinct section within the main About content column, positioned below the general company narrative. It starts approximately 32–48px beneath the last paragraph of the main content, with a section heading (e.g., ‘Who we serve’) aligned to the same left edge as the main text. Under this heading, items are laid out in a multi-column grid that echoes the product grid density but with simpler card treatments: on desktop, 2–3 columns across the 8–10-column content width, each card using 16–24px internal padding and 16–24px gaps between cards both horizontally and vertically. The list occupies the full width of the content column, but still sits well inside the container margins, ensuring there’s clear space between the grid and the overlay chat panel on the far right. The vertical footprint of this section is moderate-to-tall depending on the number of customer types, but it remains visually grouped with a 24–32px gap below the grid before the certifications section begins, preserving the clean stacked-section rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, responsive grid housed within the main content column; it reflows from multi-column to single-column on smaller viewports, maintaining its left alignment and vertical stacking, without any sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The certifications list sits immediately below the customer types section, separated by a clear 24–32px vertical gap to signal a new but related area. It shares the same left-aligned section heading style and width as the previous section, reinforcing continuity down the content column. The content itself may be arranged as a grid of certification logos with captions or as a structured bullet list; in either case, it is constrained to the same 8–10-column central width, with items arranged in 2–3 columns where appropriate to mirror the product-grid-like feel of the previous section. Spacing between items is slightly tighter (around 16–20px) to give a sense of density while retaining a minimum of 24px padding around the section’s outer edges. Vertically, the section occupies the mid-lower segment of the page body, ending with a larger 32–48px gap that transitions the eye toward the standout contact CTA area below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable block aligned with the main content column; items wrap responsively but remain constrained within the central container, with no fixed or floating positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact CTA forms the visual anchor at the bottom of the About content stack. It is placed approximately 32–48px below the certifications section, occupying the full width of the main content column (again roughly 8–10 grid columns centered). Visually, it appears as a prominent card or band with a contrasting brand colour background (e.g., blue or yellow) and generous internal padding—24–32px on all sides—to maintain the low-density, high-impact feel. The primary message and button are center-aligned within the card horizontally, while the card itself remains aligned to the left edge of the content column. On larger screens, this section occupies about one-quarter of the above-the-fold height if scrolled into view, making it noticeable but not overwhelming. Surrounding whitespace at the top and bottom (24–32px) isolates it from adjacent sections, helping it stand out as the key action after learning about the company, while keeping enough right margin for the floating WhatsApp button to sit comfortably without overlapping critical CTA content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable section at the bottom of the About content; it does not stick or float, but its strong colour block and central alignment give it a pseudo-footer presence within the page body.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart summary is integrated into the yellow main header band, aligned with the rightmost area of the centered header container. Horizontally, it sits in the top-right corner of the container, in line with the primary navigation items but separated by 24–32px of horizontal space to visually group it as a utility element rather than a navigation link. Vertically, it is centered within the height of the yellow header (approximately 64–80px high), ensuring comfortable click targets and alignment with the logo and nav. The cart summary content (icon, item count, and possibly subtotal) is compact, occupying about 15–20% of the header’s inner width and leaving ample space so the top area doesn’t feel crowded. On wide viewports, it participates in the top Z-pattern: after viewing the logo and navigation, the eye naturally lands on this right-aligned summary before moving down the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed within the global header region, which itself stays at the top of the viewport during scroll (if the site uses a sticky header); the cart summary remains visible as part of that header and does not overlap the main About content or the right-side chat widget.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The URL state sync component has no direct visual footprint but conceptually lives alongside the internal section navigation and main content sections. It ensures that as users click on ‘Overview’, ‘Who we serve’, or ‘Certifications’ in the section navigation—or scroll to those sections—the URL reflects the active section (e.g., via hash or query parameters). This alignment keeps the perceived structure of the page in sync: the highlighted tab in the horizontal navigation row corresponds to the visible section in the vertically stacked content column, and the browser’s address bar mirrors that state for sharing and re-entry.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; it does not occupy space in the layout or affect positioning. It operates logically in the background, updating URL and scroll position, while the visible layout remains governed by the header, hero band, and content column.

name: Contact
title: Contact
description: Page for contacting sales or technical support, showing contact details and providing a structured inquiry form.

core_features: Display dynamic contact information (emails, phones, address, support hours, response-time expectations) using getContactPageContent.
Show a lightweight cart summary in the header using getCartSummary, so users can see current item count and subtotal while contacting support.
Provide a contact form to submit inquiries about products, orders, or technical issues using submitContactForm.
Pre-fill and contextualize the contact form based on optional URL parameters topic and order_reference.
Show submission status feedback (success/failure) and an optional ticket ID after calling submitContactForm, without leaving the page.
user_workflows:
View contact details only:
1. User navigates to contact.html (with or without URL parameters).
2. Page initializes and calls getContactPageContent to retrieve emails, phones, address, support hours, and response-time expectation.
3. Page calls getCartSummary to show the current item_count and subtotal in the header cart area.
4. User reads the displayed contact information and may copy email/phone details to contact the company directly outside the website.

Submit a general inquiry without preset topic:
1. User opens contact.html without topic or order_reference parameters.
2. Page calls getContactPageContent and getCartSummary.
3. Contact form initializes with an implementation-defined default topic and empty order_reference.
4. User fills in name, email, optional phone, selects an appropriate topic from the form control, and writes their message.
5. User clicks the form submit button.
6. contact-form component triggers submitContactForm with the current form values (name, email, phone, topic, order_reference, message).
7. While waiting for the response, the form submission control is disabled, and a “submitting” state is shown.
8. When the response is received:
   - If success is true: contact-form-status displays the success message and ticket_id; the form may clear or lock fields based on implementation.
   - If success is false: contact-form-status shows the returned message as an error and keeps the form values for correction and resubmission.

Submit an order-related inquiry from Help page:
1. User is on help.html and chooses an option like “Contact us about this order,” which navigates to contact.html with topic and order_reference parameters (e.g., contact.html?topic=order&order_reference={reference_from_help}).
2. contact.html reads topic and order_reference from the URL.
3. Page calls getContactPageContent and getCartSummary.
4. Contact form initializes:
   - Topic field is preselected based on the topic URL parameter when it matches a supported topic value.
   - Order reference field is pre-filled with the order_reference parameter value.
5. User verifies or adjusts the topic, optionally edits the order reference, and fills in remaining fields (name, email, phone, message).
6. User submits the form, which calls submitContactForm with the filled values.
7. On success, the user sees a success message, a ticket ID, and may note this ID for future tracking; the URL remains unchanged, keeping the topic and order_reference parameters intact.

Submit a technical support inquiry from policy/FAQ pages:
1. User is on shipping.html, privacy.html, or terms.html and clicks a contextual link such as “Contact technical support,” which opens contact.html with topic set appropriately (e.g., contact.html?topic=technical_support) but without an order_reference.
2. contact.html reads the topic parameter.
3. Page calls getContactPageContent and getCartSummary.
4. Contact form initializes with the topic field preselected based on the URL parameter and an empty order reference.
5. User provides name, email, optional phone, and detailed technical message.
6. User clicks submit; submitContactForm is invoked with the current form state.
7. On success, a success message and ticket_id are displayed; user can optionally copy this information or print the page.

Check cart status while on the contact page:
1. User navigates to contact.html from any other page.
2. Page calls getCartSummary during initialization.
3. Header cart-summary-header component displays the current item_count and subtotal with currency.
4. If the user clicks the cart area (implementation: link to cart page), they can leave the contact page to review or edit their cart; the contact form state is not preserved unless handled via server or URL parameters (not required by this specification).
interactions:
Header cart summary interactions:
- On page load, cart-summary-header calls getCartSummary.
- User may click the cart icon/summary to navigate to the cart page (navigation target defined elsewhere).

Contact information interactions:
- On page load, contact-info-panel calls getContactPageContent.
- User can interact with displayed phone numbers or email addresses (e.g., clicking to trigger tel: or mailto: links if implemented), but no additional API calls are required.
- User may use address information to open external map services (handled via regular links, not additional interfaces).

URL parameter-driven form initialization:
- When contact.html loads, a URL parsing routine reads topic and order_reference parameters.
- If topic is present and matches a known topic option, the contact-form component initializes its topic field to this value; otherwise, it uses an implementation-defined default.
- If order_reference is present, the contact-form component pre-fills the order reference field with that value.

Form field interactions:
- onChange for each input (name, email, phone, topic selector, order_reference, message) updates the internal state of the contact-form component.
- Basic client-side validation can be performed onChange or onBlur (e.g., checking that email and message are non-empty); details are implementation-defined and do not require additional interfaces.
- If client-side validation fails, the submit button may be disabled or an inline error state is shown; no API calls occur at this stage.

Form submission interactions:
- onSubmit on the contact-form component:
  1. Prevents default browser form submission.
  2. Validates required fields (at minimum: name, email, topic, message; exact rules are implementation-dependent).
  3. If validation passes, calls submitContactForm with the current field values (name, email, phone, topic, order_reference, message).
  4. While waiting for the response, sets an internal "submitting" state, disabling the submit button and optionally showing a loading indicator.
- When submitContactForm returns:
  - If success is true:
    * contact-form-status component displays the returned message and ticket_id.
    * contact-form may reset its fields or mark them as read-only; behavior is implementation-defined.
  - If success is false:
    * contact-form-status displays the returned message as an error.
    * contact-form leaves user input intact to allow correction and resubmission.

Submission feedback interactions:
- User can read or copy the displayed ticket_id from contact-form-status for later reference.
- If multiple submissions occur in one session, contact-form-status updates to reflect the latest submitContactForm response.
page_functionality.state_logic:
URL parameters and in-page state control how the contact page behaves without using localStorage or similar client-side storage.

URL parameters:
- topic (string):
  - Parsed from contact.html?topic=... on initial page load.
  - The value is passed to the contact-form component as its initial selected topic when it matches a supported topic option.
  - If the parameter is absent or does not match any supported option, the contact-form uses an implementation-defined default topic.
  - The topic URL parameter does not change automatically when the user manually changes the topic field; the form state is maintained in-memory only.

- order_reference (string):
  - Parsed from contact.html?order_reference=... on initial page load.
  - The value is passed to the contact-form component as the initial value of the order reference field.
  - If absent, the initial order reference field is empty.
  - User changes to this field affect only the form state and are not written back to the URL.

Page load state sequence:
1. Parse topic and order_reference from the current URL.
2. Trigger getContactPageContent and getCartSummary in parallel.
3. When getContactPageContent resolves, populate contact-info-panel fields and display support_hours and response_time_expectation.
4. When getCartSummary resolves, update cart-summary-header with item_count, subtotal, and currency.
5. Initialize contact-form with:
   - topic: from URL (if valid) or an implementation-defined default.
   - order_reference: from URL (if present) or empty.
   - name, email, phone, message: empty.

Submission state:
- Before submit: form has a neutral state; contact-form-status is empty or hidden.
- Submitting: a transient "submitting" state is set, disabling the submit control and preventing duplicate submits.
- After submit:
  - On success (success = true):
    * A "success" state is set in the contact-form component and contact-form-status.
    * The page does not navigate away; the URL remains unchanged (no new parameters are added or removed).
  - On error (success = false):
    * An "error" state is set that includes the returned message for display.
    * The page still does not navigate or alter URL parameters.

Error handling state:
- If getContactPageContent or getCartSummary fail, components can set an internal "load_error" state for their respective sections and show a fallback error message based on implementation-defined behavior (no additional interfaces). These errors do not alter URL parameters.

No persistent client-side storage:
- All page state (form values, loading flags, error/success states) is kept in component memory for the current session and is reset on full page reload or navigation.
- URL parameters are the only mechanism for pre-configuring the form from other pages.

components.functionality: Initializes the Contact page, orchestrates data fetching (contact details and cart summary), parses URL parameters, and passes data and initial state into child components.
Displays a compact cart summary (item count and subtotal) in the page header and allows navigation to the cart page.
Shows dynamic contact information including support and sales emails, phone numbers, address lines, support hours, and response-time expectations.
Provides inputs for name, email, phone, topic, order reference, and message, initializes them from URL parameters where applicable, validates them, and submits inquiries via submitContactForm.
Displays the outcome of contact form submissions, including success or error messages and any returned ticket ID.
Reflects the current inquiry context inferred from the topic and order_reference URL parameters, helping the user confirm they are contacting the appropriate channel.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid typical of WooCommerce/WordPress themes, with content centered in a fixed-width container inside a full-bleed header.
layout.layout_pattern: Reuse the mockup’s linear top‑to‑bottom structure: full‑width utility/header/search area inside the page-shell → breadcrumb row at the top of the main content → a generous vertical gap → a centered H1 page title 'Contact' → a smaller vertical gap → a single-row horizontal context indicator (replacing the product category filter) → below that, the main contact content in a two-column layout (left contact-info-panel, right contact-form) starting from the left edge of the content container. The cart-summary-header appears as a slim, right-aligned bar on the same horizontal band as or just below the breadcrumb, and the contact-form-status lives directly with the form, not as a separate section. As in the mockup, a chat panel and WhatsApp FAB sit above everything as overlay elements, defined by the page-shell.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Reuse the mockup’s linear top‑to‑bottom structure: full‑width utility/header/search area inside the page-shell → breadcrumb row at the top of the main content → a generous vertical gap → a centered H1 page title 'Contact' → a smaller vertical gap → a single-row horizontal context indicator (replacing the product category filter) → below that, the main contact content in a two-column layout (left contact-info-panel, right contact-form) starting from the left edge of the content container. The cart-summary-header appears as a slim, right-aligned bar on the same horizontal band as or just below the breadcrumb, and the contact-form-status lives directly with the form, not as a separate section. As in the mockup, a chat panel and WhatsApp FAB sit above everything as overlay elements, defined by the page-shell.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header band (black utility + yellow main header + search/category bar) occupies roughly the top 20–25% of the viewport, full width. The breadcrumb + title + context indicator area takes another ~20–25%, with deliberate vertical spacing: 16–24px around the breadcrumb, ~32–48px between breadcrumb and H1, and ~24px between H1 and context indicator. The lower 50–60% of the viewport is dedicated to the contact content: the left contact-info-panel uses about 4–5 of 12 grid columns (roughly 35–40% of the content width), and the right contact-form uses about 7–8 columns (60–65%), both inside a centered fixed-width container with generous left/right margins. The cart-summary-header is a compact strip occupying roughly the rightmost 30–40% of the content width, visually light. The chat panel and WhatsApp FAB consume a narrow vertical slice on the far right without affecting layout flow.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain a low-density, breathable feel in the upper and mid sections: single-row elements (breadcrumb, title, context indicator) with generous vertical gaps and minimal competing content. The two-column contact section is denser but still orderly: the contact-info-panel groups key details with moderate spacing, while the contact-form is the densest area (form fields stacked with 16–24px vertical spacing). Overall, information is structured so that the user first understands context (where they are, what type of contact) and then focuses on completing the form, mirroring the product page’s shift from airy intro to denser product grid.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Preserve the desktop Z‑pattern: the eye begins at the top-left logo/brand in the black/yellow header, moves right across navigation and cart, then drops diagonally down to the left-side breadcrumb at the start of the main content. From there it moves right across the row to the cart-summary-header. Next, the gaze drops to the large centered 'Contact' H1, then across the horizontal contact-context-indicator row. From there, the eye moves down to the left starting at the contact-info-panel, then scans right to the contact-form, and finally downward through the form fields. The floating chat widget and WhatsApp button on the right act as secondary focal points, periodically pulling attention but not interrupting the main left-to-right, top-to-bottom reading flow.

layout.overall_description: The Contact page sits inside the same clean, corporate e‑commerce shell as the product listing: a full-width black utility strip and bold yellow header with logo and navigation span the top of the viewport, followed immediately by a horizontal search/category bar. Beneath this, the main content is centered in a fixed-width container with generous left/right margins. At the very top of this container is a slim breadcrumb row, left-aligned, with a light, right-aligned cart-summary-header on the same line or just below it, echoing the product page’s light midband content. A sizeable band of whitespace separates this from a large, centered 'Contact' title that visually anchors the page. Directly under the title, a single horizontal row of context options (the contact-context-indicator) stretches across the central portion of the page, resembling the single-line category filter from the mockup. Below this, the layout transitions into a two-column grid: the left column holds the contact-info-panel (address, phone, email, support information), aligned to start at the left edge of the content column, while the right, slightly wider column contains the structured contact-form, stacked fields and a primary submit button. The form’s feedback (contact-form-status) appears within or directly beneath the form, maintaining proximity to the user’s action. Overlaid on the right side of the viewport, independent of scroll, is the blue chat panel, and at the bottom-right corner floats the green WhatsApp button, both adding interactive presence without disturbing the clean, breathable vertical flow of the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-shell defines the full viewport structure and background. At the very top, spanning 100% of the viewport width, is a shallow black utility bar (about 40–56px tall) containing the logo or small brand mark on the left and secondary utilities (e.g., sign-in, help) aligned to the right. Directly beneath it, also full width, is a taller yellow main header band (roughly 72–96px tall) aligned to the same fixed-width central container: the logo appears at the far left of this container, primary navigation links occupy the center/left, and account/cart icons sit at the right. Below the yellow header is the search/category bar: a horizontal band about 64–80px tall, with a 'Browse Categories' button aligned left inside the central container, and a large search input extending towards the center/right, both following the 12‑column grid and 8px spacing system. The shell then introduces the main content area: a white or very light background, with the core content constrained to a centered fixed-width container (e.g., 1140–1280px) aligned to the grid. This main content container hosts the breadcrumb row, cart-summary-header, the contact title, the contact-context-indicator, and the two-column layout for contact-info-panel and contact-form. On top of all content, the page-shell also positions a right-aligned chat widget: a rectangular blue chat panel fixed to the right side of the viewport, mid-height (centered vertically, overlapping the main content but not obscuring the header), with a small inset from the right edge (~24–32px). At the bottom-right corner of the viewport, about 24–32px from both the bottom and right edges, floats a circular green WhatsApp button (floating action button), sitting above the chat panel in z-order and independent of scroll. All these elements adhere to the 8px spacing scale with clear, calm separations between sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Defines structural layout; header and content scroll normally. Chat panel is fixed to the right side of the viewport, vertically centered, and remains visible on scroll. WhatsApp FAB is fixed to bottom-right of the viewport and remains visible on scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart-summary-header is a compact horizontal block that appears in the upper part of the main content container, closely aligned with the breadcrumb row to echo the product page’s light midband. It sits either on the same horizontal line as the breadcrumb, aligned to the right of the container, or on a row immediately below it with a very tight vertical gap (8–16px), maintaining the perception of a single band. It occupies roughly the rightmost 30–40% of the content width (about 4–5 of 12 grid columns) and is right-aligned, leaving clear space between it and the breadcrumb on the left. Visually, it resembles a subtle summary chip or mini-banner: a light background, small cart icon and text like '2 items in cart', with 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, adhering to the 8px grid. Its height is modest (around 40–56px), so it doesn’t compete visually with the large H1 below. A vertical gap of approximately 32–40px separates this cart summary band from the centered 'Contact' title beneath, preserving the airy midsection feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrolls with the main content as part of the top-of-content band; no sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-info-panel resides in the main two-column section beneath the context indicator. The two-column section begins after a vertical gap of roughly 32px below the contact-context-indicator. Within the fixed-width content container, the contact-info-panel anchors to the left edge and spans about 4–5 of the 12 grid columns (approximately 35–40% of the content width). It starts higher on the page than the form’s content, so that its top edge horizontally aligns with the form’s heading or the first form fields, maintaining a cohesive row. The panel itself is a vertically stacked card-like block: a subtle bordered or lightly shaded rectangle with 16–24px padding on all sides, containing blocks for address, phone, email, business hours, and possibly sales/support contacts. Each item in the panel is separated by 16–24px vertical spacing to maintain clarity. Icons or labels align to the left, keeping the text ragged-right, and the overall height of the panel is moderate—typically ending somewhere between 50–75% of the form’s height, so the user sees both columns concurrently without one dominating vertically. The left column’s baseline alignment matches the form column, reinforcing the grid structure and giving a solid visual starting point for the eye in this section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolls with the page as part of the main two-column content; no sticky behavior on its own.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-form is the main interactive focal area and occupies the right column of the two-column layout. Starting at the same vertical position as the contact-info-panel, it anchors to the right side of the central content container and spans roughly 7–8 of the 12 grid columns (about 60–65% of the content width). The form is a stacked sequence of inputs arranged in a single column: a form title or short introduction at the top (aligned left), followed by labeled fields (e.g., name, email, phone, company, type of inquiry, message), each with 8–12px spacing between label and input, and 16–24px vertical spacing between input groups. The overall form block may sit on a subtle card background or directly on the page background, but it respects 16–24px internal padding from any card edges. The primary submit button is left-aligned to the form’s content, located near the bottom with ~24px of space above it from the last field, and ~16–24px below if a status/secondary help text appears under the button. The form’s top aligns horizontally with the start of the contact-info-panel, and the right edge aligns with the container’s grid, leaving a comfortable margin to the viewport edge. On larger screens, the form’s height will typically extend lower than the contact-info-panel, leading the eye downward into the densest interactive part of the page while maintaining alignment with the 8px spacing system.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolls with the page. All elements are laid out in a normal document flow, with no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-form-status is a compact feedback panel tightly coupled to the contact-form. It appears inside the form’s right column, not as a separate full-width block. When visible, it is placed directly above the form fields or directly below the form’s title/intro, spanning the same width as the form’s input column. It takes the form of a colored notification bar or card (e.g., subtle green for success, red/orange for error), with 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, following the 8px spacing scale. The panel is aligned left within the form and does not exceed the form’s width; its right edge aligns with the right edge of the inputs. A small vertical gap of about 16px separates this status panel from the nearest form field below so the relationship is clear but not cramped. If used for inline validation at submit time, additional micro-messages may appear directly under specific fields, but the main contact-form-status panel remains near the top of the form, ensuring the user sees feedback without scrolling. It does not modify the placement of the contact-info-panel or any other section; it simply shifts the form’s fields slightly downward when present.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears dynamically within the normal flow of the contact-form; pushing subsequent form fields down when visible but otherwise not affecting overall layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-context-indicator sits in the mid-page area where the product page originally showed a horizontal category filter. It is positioned directly beneath the centered 'Contact' H1, separated by a vertical gap of about 24–32px. The indicator is a single horizontal row of text options or pill-style buttons (e.g., 'General Inquiry', 'Sales', 'Technical Support') centered within the content container. The entire row spans roughly 60–80% of the content width, leaving comfortable margins on both sides, and each item has 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding to feel clickable without being bulky. Items are laid out inline, left-to-right, with 16px spacing between them, and the currently active context is highlighted using the brand’s blue or yellow, similar to the selected category in the product filter. The row’s visual weight is lighter than the H1 but more prominent than the breadcrumb, creating a clear mid-level control. A vertical gap of about 32px separates this context row from the two-column contact section below, creating a distinct transitional band that matches the open, breathable midsection of the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static row within the main content, scrolls with the page; items may have hover/active states but no sticky behavior.

name: Help & FAQ
title: Help & FAQ
description: Help center page that loads FAQ sections, allows users to browse or search answers about ordering, shipping, returns, payment methods, site features (filters, comparison, cart), and high-level product selection guidance, with quick access to contact support and the current cart summary.

core_features: Load and display structured Help & FAQ content by section using getHelpFaqContent.
Highlight a focused FAQ section based on the optional `section` URL parameter for deep-linking to topics like ordering, shipping, returns, or product selection.
Allow users to switch between FAQ sections (e.g., ordering, shipping, returns, filters & comparison, product selection) via in-page navigation without reloading the entire page.
Provide an in-page FAQ text filter that narrows visible questions and answers across the loaded content without additional API calls.
Show contextual 'Contact support' actions on FAQ entries that navigate to contact.html with a topic derived from the selected question or section.
Provide contextual navigation from shipping-related FAQs to shipping.html for detailed shipping and returns information.
Display a lightweight cart summary in the page header using getCartSummary, showing current cart item count and subtotal, and linking to the full cart view.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Arrive on Help & FAQ without a section parameter
1. User navigates to help.html.
2. The page controller component calls getHelpFaqContent to retrieve all help sections and FAQs.
3. The page controller component calls getCartSummary to retrieve current cart item count and subtotal for header display.
4. Once getHelpFaqContent resolves, the page determines the default active section (for example, the first section returned) because no `section` parameter is present.
5. The section navigation component renders a tab or list entry for each returned section and highlights the active one.
6. The FAQ list component renders the questions and answers for the active section.
7. The user can expand/collapse questions (if accordions are used), read answers, and use the FAQ search bar to filter questions without leaving the page.
8. At any time, the user can click the cart summary to navigate away to the cart page (e.g., cart.html).

Workflow: Deep-link to a specific topic via URL parameter
1. User opens a direct link such as help.html?section=<sectionKey> (the actual key corresponds to one of the section keys returned by getHelpFaqContent).
2. The page controller parses the `section` parameter from the URL.
3. The page controller calls getHelpFaqContent and getCartSummary.
4. After getHelpFaqContent returns, the controller searches the sections array for one whose key matches the URL `section` value.
5. If a matching section exists, it is set as the active section; otherwise, a default section (for example, the first one returned) becomes active.
6. The section navigation shows all sections with the matched section highlighted.
7. The FAQ list component renders only the active section’s FAQs. The user can read or filter within this section or switch sections, which updates the URL `section` parameter accordingly.

Workflow: Contact support from a specific FAQ entry
1. User scrolls to a relevant FAQ in the active section.
2. The FAQ list component shows a 'Contact support about this question' action associated with that FAQ entry.
3. On click, an event handler derives a `topic` string from the selected FAQ (for example, based on the section key and/or question text, transformed into a URL-safe value, without hardcoding any specific topics).
4. The browser navigates to contact.html with the derived topic in the query string (e.g., contact.html?topic=<derivedTopic>).
5. On the contact page, the pre-filled topic helps route the inquiry appropriately.

Workflow: Learn more about shipping & returns
1. User views FAQs that belong to any shipping-related section (identified by its section key or metadata from getHelpFaqContent).
2. The FAQ list component or a contextual helper within that section displays a 'Learn more about shipping & returns' link.
3. When the user clicks this link, the browser navigates to shipping.html without additional parameters (or with parameters as defined elsewhere in the system, but not hardcoded here).
4. The user reads the detailed shipping and returns policy on the shipping page.

Workflow: Use Help & FAQ to understand how to use filters, comparison, and product selection
1. User navigates to help.html or a deep link pointing to a site-features or product-selection-related section.
2. The page controller loads all sections and sets the appropriate one as active via the `section` parameter or default selection.
3. In the active section, the FAQ list component presents entries that explain how to use product filters (e.g., by micron rating, pump type), comparison lists, and cart functionality at a high level.
4. The user can filter within these FAQs using the FAQ search bar (for example, by typing 'filter', 'comparison', or a product concept), which narrows the displayed questions.
5. If the user needs further assistance (e.g., selecting micron size or pump capacity), they can click 'Contact support about this question' from the relevant FAQ to go to contact.html with an appropriate topic value.

Workflow: Check cart status from the Help & FAQ page
1. When help.html loads, the page controller calls getCartSummary.
2. The cart summary header component displays current item_count and subtotal using the returned data.
3. The user reviews the cart summary while consulting FAQs about ordering, payment, or shipping.
4. If the user clicks on the cart summary area, the browser navigates to the cart page (e.g., cart.html), letting them adjust items before or after reading help content.
interactions:
Interaction: Section navigation click
- User clicks on a section tab or list item in the FAQ section navigation.
- Event handler updates the active section key in page state.
- Event handler updates the URL query string `section` parameter to the selected section’s key (e.g., help.html?section=<selectedKey>), using either a full navigation or history API.
- FAQ list re-renders to show only FAQs from the newly active section.
- If a FAQ search term is currently applied, it is re-applied to the FAQs of the newly active section without additional API calls.

Interaction: FAQ expand/collapse
- FAQ entries within the active section may be presented as collapsible items.
- When the user clicks a question row, an event toggles the expanded/collapsed state of that FAQ entry in the page state.
- Only the selected FAQ’s visibility state changes; no new data is fetched.
- The expanded state does not modify the URL and is reset on page reload.

Interaction: FAQ text search/filter
- User types in the FAQ search input field.
- On each change (or on explicit submit), an event handler updates a search query state variable.
- The FAQ list component applies a client-side filter over the questions and/or answers of either the active section or all sections (depending on configuration) using the search query.
- Only FAQs whose text matches the query are rendered; others are hidden from view.
- No new API calls are made and the URL is not modified by search input (unless optionally desired, but not required).

Interaction: 'Contact support about this question' click
- Within each FAQ entry, a 'Contact support' action is available.
- When clicked, an event handler computes a `derivedTopic` value from the FAQ context (for example, combining section key and some normalized question identifier).
- The handler triggers navigation to contact.html with `topic` URL parameter set (e.g., contact.html?topic=<derivedTopic>).
- This supports context-aware support requests without storing anything in localStorage.

Interaction: 'Learn more about shipping & returns' click
- In any section identified as shipping-related, a contextual link 'Learn more about shipping & returns' is displayed.
- When the user clicks this link, the browser navigates to shipping.html.
- No additional API calls are performed by this interaction.
- The user can later return to help.html via browser navigation to continue browsing FAQs.

Interaction: Cart summary click in header
- The cart summary header component displays a cart icon, item count, and subtotal using getCartSummary data.
- When the user clicks this area (icon or text), an event handler navigates to the cart page (e.g., cart.html).
- No Help-specific state is preserved beyond what is encoded in the URL; the user can navigate back via browser controls.

Interaction: Page load / refresh with or without section parameter
- On every load or refresh of help.html, the page controller reads the URL query parameters.
- If a `section` parameter is present, it is stored temporarily in local page state.
- The controller triggers getHelpFaqContent and getCartSummary.
- Once sections are loaded, the controller attempts to match the `section` param to a section’s key, and sets active section accordingly.
- If there is no match or no `section` parameter, a default section (for example, the first section returned) becomes active.
page_functionality.state_logic: The main page state is driven by URL parameters and API responses, without using localStorage. The key parameter is `section` in help.html. On page initialization, the controller reads `section` from the URL (e.g., help.html?section=<sectionKey>). The controller then calls getHelpFaqContent to retrieve the array of sections and their FAQs. When the response is received, the controller searches for a section whose `key` matches the URL `section` parameter. If a match is found, the active section state is set to that key; otherwise, the active section is set to a default (e.g., the first section in the returned array). When the user clicks a different section in the section navigation, the controller updates the active section state and synchronizes the URL by changing or adding the `section` parameter to the selected section’s key. This allows bookmarking and deep-linking: reloading help.html with the same `section` value restores the same active section as long as getHelpFaqContent returns a section with that key. FAQ expand/collapse states and search terms are kept in transient page state only and are not encoded in the URL. The 'Contact support' interaction constructs navigation to contact.html with a `topic` parameter (e.g., contact.html?topic=<derivedTopic>) derived at click time from the selected FAQ, without storing any additional data. The 'Learn more about shipping & returns' link navigates to shipping.html without additional parameters. Cart summary state (item_count, subtotal, currency) is entirely derived from getCartSummary on each page load or manual refresh; no cart data is stored on the Help page. Thus, the Help & FAQ page’s persistent behavior across sessions is controlled strictly via URL parameters and the dynamic API results.

components.functionality: Initializes the Help & FAQ page, coordinates data fetching from getHelpFaqContent and getCartSummary, parses URL parameters, manages active section state, and passes data to child components.
Displays a compact cart summary (icon, item count, subtotal, currency) in the header based on getCartSummary and provides navigation to the full cart page when clicked.

Renders navigation items (tabs or list) for each FAQ section returned by getHelpFaqContent, highlights the active section, and updates both internal state and the URL `section` parameter when the user selects a different section.
Allows the user to enter free-text queries to filter visible FAQs; updates a search query value in page state used by the FAQ list component to filter questions and answers client-side.

Displays FAQs for the currently active section, supporting expand/collapse per question, client-side text filtering based on the search query, contextual 'Contact support' links per FAQ, and optional 'Learn more about shipping & returns' links for shipping-related sections.

Utility logic (which can be embedded or modular) that derives a topic string from the current FAQ context (e.g., using section key and question text) and constructs the URL to contact.html with the `topic` parameter when a user clicks a 'Contact support' link.

Ensures that changes to the active FAQ section in page state are reflected in the `section` query parameter in the URL, and that initial page state is hydrated from the `section` parameter on page load or when the URL changes.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid typical of WooCommerce/WordPress themes, with content centered in a fixed-width container inside a full-bleed header.
layout.layout_pattern: Within the existing site shell (black utility bar + yellow main header + global search/category bar), the Help & FAQ page content appears as a centered, fixed-width column. Inside this column, the flow is: a top row with the page title on the left and a compact cart summary on the right → a generous vertical gap → a full-width Help search bar band (mirroring the product search bar in the mockup) → a smaller vertical gap → a single-row horizontal FAQ section navigation (category filter/tabs), centered in the content width → then the main FAQ list panel filling the remaining left side space as a dense vertical list. Overlaid on top of this flow, a right-side contact helper (FAQ contact link builder) floats as a panel in the mid-right of the viewport, with behavior similar to the chat widget, while the URL state synchronizer works invisibly and does not affect visual arrangement.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Within the existing site shell (black utility bar + yellow main header + global search/category bar), the Help & FAQ page content appears as a centered, fixed-width column. Inside this column, the flow is: a top row with the page title on the left and a compact cart summary on the right → a generous vertical gap → a full-width Help search bar band (mirroring the product search bar in the mockup) → a smaller vertical gap → a single-row horizontal FAQ section navigation (category filter/tabs), centered in the content width → then the main FAQ list panel filling the remaining left side space as a dense vertical list. Overlaid on top of this flow, a right-side contact helper (FAQ contact link builder) floats as a panel in the mid-right of the viewport, with behavior similar to the chat widget, while the URL state synchronizer works invisibly and does not affect visual arrangement.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global header and site-level search band still occupy the top ~20–25% of the viewport. The Help & FAQ page’s own header area (page title + cart summary + Help search + section navigation) uses another ~20–25%, with generous vertical spacing between these elements. The lower ~50–60% of the viewport is dedicated to the FAQ list panel, which can scroll. Horizontally, all main Help content is constrained to a central 12-column grid with ~80–90% of the page width, leaving comfortable left and right margins. The cart summary uses the upper-right quadrant of this content column, roughly 3–4 of 12 columns. The floating contact helper and any quick-contact icon occupy a slim vertical slice on the far right (visually 10–15% of the viewport width) but are overlayed so they do not push content.
layout_strategies.content_density: Echoing the mockup, the density is low at the top and higher in the main content area. The title row and cart summary are clean with ample whitespace and only a few key elements. The Help search bar sits in an uncluttered band with comfortable padding (16–24px vertically), giving it strong focus. FAQ section navigation is a single row of clear, spaced category labels or pills. The FAQ list itself is more content-dense with stacked questions/answers, but line heights and paddings still align to the 8px spacing system to keep readability high. The floating contact helper is compact but visually strong, similar in information density to the chat widget in the mockup (short text + 1–2 buttons/links).
layout_strategies.visual_flow: On desktop, the eye follows a Z-pattern similar to the product listing mockup: starting top-left at the 'Help & FAQ' title, moving right across to the cart summary header, then diagonally down to the left side where the Help search bar starts, scanning right along the search input and controls. From there, the eye drops down to the FAQ section navigation row (All / Ordering / Shipping / Returns / etc.) and then flows left-to-right across these categories. Finally, the gaze moves downward into the FAQ list panel, scanning vertically through questions. The floating contact helper on the right edge and any contact CTA at the bottom-right act as secondary focal points that attract attention after the main reading path, similar to the chat panel and WhatsApp FAB in the original mockup.

layout.overall_description: Beneath the existing site header and global search/category bar, the Help & FAQ page appears as a clean, centered content area inside a fixed-width container following a 12-column grid. At the top of this area, the 'Help & FAQ' page title anchors the left side, while a compact cart summary widget aligns to the right, forming a balanced, low-density header row with generous top and bottom padding. Below this, a wide Help search bar band stretches almost the full content width, mirroring the e-commerce search strip from the mockup, with a prominent search field centered and perhaps a 'Browse topics' affordance to its left. A clear vertical gap follows, then a single horizontal strip of FAQ section navigation (category filters) sits in the center, offering quick access to different help topics. Below that, the main FAQ list panel begins near the left edge of the content column, filling most of the width with vertically stacked FAQ accordions or question blocks. While users scroll through FAQs, a slim, right-aligned floating contact helper panel hovers midway down the viewport, slightly inset from the edge, echoing the chat widget’s position in the mockup and providing quick links to contact support or escalate issues. Overall, the page breathes at the top and becomes more information-dense as users move downward, maintaining the clean, corporate e-commerce feel with strong brand colors used sparingly for emphasis.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The help-page-controller acts as the structural wrapper for all Help & FAQ content below the global site header and the site-level search/category bar. It defines a centered main content column constrained to a fixed max-width (e.g., 1120–1200px) aligned to a 12-column grid, with equal left and right margins on large screens. Inside this container, it applies the vertical rhythm consistent with the mockup: a 32–48px gap from the global header area to the page-title row, 24–32px between the title row and the Help search bar, 24px between the search bar and the FAQ section navigation, and 24–32px between the section navigation and the FAQ list panel. All child components (cart-summary-header, faq-search-bar, faq-section-navigation, faq-list-panel) are arranged in a linear top-to-bottom flow, with the cart summary sharing the top row horizontally with the title. The wrapper ensures that the FAQ list panel can scroll within the page while floating elements (like the contact helper) overlay it without shifting the layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level container that scrolls with the page; provides the 12-column grid and vertical spacing rules but does not itself float or stick.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart-summary-header appears in the topmost row of the Help & FAQ content area, aligned to the right side of the main content column. Visually, this row is divided into two segments: the left ~70% (about 8 of 12 grid columns) for the 'Help & FAQ' page title and optional short subtitle, and the right ~30% (about 4 of 12 columns) for the cart summary. The cart summary sits flush to the right edge of the container, horizontally aligned with the baseline of the page title text. It is a compact, light-weight block—perhaps a small panel showing cart icon, item count, and total—no taller than 56–72px, with 12–16px of internal padding. There is at least 24–32px of vertical whitespace above and below this row, giving it the airy feeling seen around headings in the mockup. On narrower screens, this component stacks below the title (still right-aligned within the container), but on desktop it stays side-by-side, maintaining the Z-pattern from title (left) to cart (right).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element within the top content row; remains part of normal document flow and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The faq-section-navigation sits below the Help search bar, occupying a single horizontal strip across the central content area, similar to the product category filter row in the mockup. It is centered horizontally in the main content column and spans roughly 80–100% of the container width, with a max-width matching the FAQ list panel for alignment. The navigation items (e.g., All, Ordering, Shipping, Returns, Payment, Site Features, Product Guidance) are laid out in a single row of text links or pill-style filters. Each item has equal horizontal padding (12–16px) and is separated by 16–24px of space, creating a loose, breathable feel. The entire row has 12–16px of vertical padding and sits on a neutral background (e.g., white or very light grey) so that the active item, highlighted with brand colors, stands out while maintaining a clean look. There is a 24px vertical gap above (from the bottom edge of the FAQ search bar) and 24–32px below (before the FAQ list panel), echoing the generous spacing between filter and product grid in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static horizontal navigation bar that scrolls with the page. Optionally, on very tall pages it could become sticky at the top of the help content, but by default it behaves as part of the normal flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The faq-search-bar occupies a prominent band immediately beneath the top title/cart row, mirroring the full-width search/category bar in the mockup. It is a horizontally oriented strip that stretches across nearly the entire content width (about 90–100% of the container), with 16–24px of vertical padding inside and 24–32px of clear whitespace above and below. Inside this band, the left side (about 25–30% of the strip) can hold a 'Browse topics' label or compact dropdown for high-level topic groups, while the central/right region (about 60–70% of the strip) is taken by a large search input field. The search input is horizontally centered within the band and aligned so that its left edge forms a vertical line with the Help title and FAQ list panel content below. The input itself has comfortable padding (10–14px vertically, 16–24px horizontally) and spans roughly 60% of the container width on desktop, creating a strong focal element similar in importance to the product search in the mockup. The entire search bar band uses subtle background contrast (slightly tinted or bordered) to distinguish it as a functional control area while retaining the clean, flat visual style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width control band within the help-page-controller; scrolls with content and does not overlap other sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The faq-list-panel forms the main content area below the FAQ section navigation, analogous to the product grid in the mockup. It is left-aligned within the main content container, with its content starting on the same left grid line as the page title and the search input above, reinforcing a strong vertical alignment. The panel spans approximately 8–10 of the 12 grid columns (roughly 70–85% of the container width), leaving a modest margin to the right that visually accommodates the floating contact helper without making the list feel cramped. Inside this panel, FAQ items are stacked vertically as expandable accordions or grouped sections, with each FAQ block separated by 16–24px of vertical spacing. The top of the faq-list-panel begins 24–32px below the FAQ section navigation row, keeping the upper area comfortably spaced. As users scroll, this panel becomes the densest region of the page, showing multiple questions on screen at once yet maintaining a 1.5–1.7 line-height and consistent 8–16px paddings for readability. On very long content, the panel extends down the page, while the rest of the layout (header/title/search/navigation) scrolls off-screen, similar to how the product grid continues below the fold in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Primary scrolling content block; static within the flow, fully scrollable, and designed to coexist with an overlaid right-side contact helper without reflow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The faq-contact-link-builder is positioned as an overlaid helper on the right side of the viewport, inspired by the chat widget and WhatsApp FAB in the mockup. On desktop, it appears as a compact vertical panel floating over the main content, anchored approximately midway down the viewport height (around 40–60% from the top) and inset 24–32px from the right edge of the window. Its width is relatively narrow (about 260–320px, roughly 15–20% of the viewport on large screens), and it does not push or reflow the FAQ list panel; instead, the underlying content can scroll behind it. The panel contains a short, clear header (e.g., 'Need more help?'), a brief line of explanatory text, and 1–3 prominent action links or buttons (e.g., 'Contact Support', 'Start Chat', 'Email Us'), arranged vertically with 12–16px gaps. Internal padding around the content is 16–24px. The background uses a strong brand color (blue or accented with yellow) to create contrast, similar to the blue chat widget in the mockup, while text and icons are kept simple and flat. On the bottom-right corner of the viewport, optionally, a small circular quick-contact button (e.g., WhatsApp-style) can be docked 24px from the bottom and right edges, aligning visually with this panel and serving as a tertiary, always-visible CTA.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Floating, overlayed element with a fixed or sticky position relative to the viewport on desktop. It remains visible as the user scrolls the FAQ list, without affecting the layout of other components. On smaller screens, it can collapse into a single floating button to reduce obstruction.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-state-synchronizer is non-visual and occupies no explicit space in the layout. Conceptually, it is attached to the help-page-controller and listens to interactions from the faq-section-navigation, faq-search-bar, and faq-list-panel (e.g., selected category, search query, opened question) to update the URL and restore state. From a layout perspective, it is best thought of as a background behavior tied to the page, ensuring smooth, URL-based navigation without any visible footprint or displacement of content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Invisible, no spatial behavior in the rendered UI; logically positioned at the page level and scrolling with the document but without occupying any space.

name: Shipping & Returns
title: Shipping & Returns
description: Provides dynamic information about shipping methods, delivery coverage, free shipping criteria, and returns/fees, and lets users navigate directly to the relevant section from help or checkout pages.

core_features: Fetch and display shipping and returns policy content (overview, free shipping criteria, delivery areas, returns policy, restocking fees) using getShippingAndReturnsContent.
Fetch and list all available shipping methods, including cost and delivery time information, using getShippingMethods.
Show a compact cart summary in the page header using getCartSummary so users can see their current subtotal and item count while reviewing shipping policies.
Support deep-link navigation via the section URL parameter so users can land directly on specific sections such as free_shipping or returns.
Provide contextual navigation to related pages: shipping FAQs (help.html), returns contact (contact.html), and returning to checkout (checkout.html).

Explain how free shipping criteria are applied and how the Product.free_shipping attribute affects product listing badges and filters (described using policy text from getShippingAndReturnsContent, not hardcoded).
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Reviewing shipping methods from checkout
1) User is at checkout.html and clicks a link such as “View shipping options and policy”.
2) User is redirected to shipping.html, optionally with a section parameter (e.g., section=methods or section=free_shipping).
3) On page load, the page reads the section parameter and calls getShippingAndReturnsContent, getShippingMethods, and getCartSummary in parallel.
4) Once data loads, the section-scroll handler scrolls to the section corresponding to the section parameter (for example, the free shipping or shipping methods section).
5) User reads the applicable shipping methods, including names, descriptions, base costs, and estimated delivery times rendered from getShippingMethods.
6) User uses the in-page navigation to jump between sections if needed.
7) When finished, the user clicks the “Back to checkout” link, which navigates to checkout.html (without adding extra parameters from this page).

Workflow 2: Understanding free shipping rules for product listings
1) User clicks the “Shipping & Returns” link in the footer from any page and opens shipping.html without a section parameter.
2) The page loads, calls getShippingAndReturnsContent, getShippingMethods, and getCartSummary, and by default scrolls to the top (overview section).
